concepts.title: Chi and the Eumezu
concepts.description: Chi as personal divine spark; Isumu four-part self (Chi/E-Ezu/Aka-Chiwa/unknown); Aka (refined) vs. Awa (impulse); fourth tradition on dual-faculty governance; agwu/Chi relationship

concepts.title: Aru and Iwuala
concepts.description: two-tier law: Aru (cosmic) vs. Iwuala (community); Ofọ as personal law; sorcery as Aru-register disruption; symbolic release as cosmological repair

concepts.title: Ancestral Practice in Odinala
concepts.description: learn ancestors' names and stories; speak with authority as living representative; Ubi as cosmogram; agwu initiation for healing-called practitioners

concepts.title: Igbo Ancestral Psychology
concepts.description: multi-causal psychology; six Ara causes; five named subtypes (Ajoihe, Soa, Umaku, Isa, Aun); OFA/Iwa/Buru-buru actualization framework; Ara as personified entity (renegotiation, not elimination)

concepts.title: Igbo Dibia Taxonomy
concepts.description: 14-type healer classification; Dibia as genius/Abiya custodian; Agwu as external selection deity; Ara Aun enforcement; three-body healing (physical/social/cosmic)

concepts.title: Symbolic Release and Tying Rituals
concepts.description: full treatment protocol: Raia → Ani → Guata → Machino (renegotiation) → Dayong walk → music/dance → Noala; cure-as-renegotiation

concepts.title: Popobawa Collective Psychosis
concepts.description: Sleep paralysis neurobiological mechanism underlying collective psychosis epidemics; culture-bound syndrome framework; Popobawa epidemic in Tanzania/East Africa; nocebo amplification through cultural interpretation; anxiety-driven sleep disruption feedback loop

concepts.title: Juju Oaths and Magical Control
concepts.description: Supernatural belief as control mechanism for human trafficking in West Africa; belief in supernatural consequence producing psychosomatic illness; internalization, invisibility, universality, transferability as mechanisms; nocebo effect activation through belief

concepts.title: Oba Symbolic Authority Revocation
concepts.description: March 9, 2018 Oba of Lagos declaration revoking all juju oaths; trafficking rates dropping 72% → 2% within 3 years; proof that consensual reality can be inverted by sufficient authority; supernatural control systems as collective agreements
